hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: Buyers are flooding in on the YES side, betting Congress gets its act together before the deadline, a 10-point spike that signals real optimism about a deal after weeks of brinkmanship theater. At 55.8%, the market's pricing in a coin flip when history says DHS shutdowns resolve faster than most funding fights, making YES the sharper play here.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Jump 10 Points. Market Pricing In What Congress Hasn't Said Yet.
daily_take.deck: The DHS resolution odds vaulted from 45.7% to 55.8% overnight, a 10-point swing on $714K volume. This moves faster than legislative reality typically allows, suggesting traders are front-running either a backroom deal or a breakdown in talks. If you're holding this at 56%, you're betting Congress finds a path by March 31. The smarter play is watching the 72-hour legislative calendar. If the deadline passes without a framework, this collapses back to 30%.

sidebar.headline: CDU collapse is overcorrected. Down 15 points in 24 hours after internal polling, but German voters punish dysfunction slower than markets price it.
sidebar.label: CDU Most Seats, Rhineland-Palatinate: 54.0%

sidebar.headline: Jaylen Wells rebounds climbing hard. Someone just loaded the over at 50%. Check if the Grizzlies adjusted his role this week.

sidebar.headline: Anduril IPO odds cratered 22 points. Defense contractors get delayed in crowded windows. This 37.5% might be the floor if the broader market stabilizes.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO Before 2027: 37.5%
